sections.heading: Manage Views in the Data Explorer
Collections Screen
sections.body:
The Collections screen lists the existing
[check](atlas-ui-collections) and
[views](atlas-ui-views) in the selected database. Each
list item includes the name and other general information for
the collection or view.

To access the Collections screen for a database, from the
[Databases screen](atlas-ui-database-tab) either:

- Click a Database Name in the main Databases
  view, or

- Click a database in the left navigation.

![Select database](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/select-database.png)

Atlas displays views in the **Collections Screen**
with a special icon, and indicates the collection from which the view
was created.

sections.heading: View Information
sections.body:
The **Collections** screen displays the following information
for each view in the selected database:

- View name

- Collection from which the view was created

sections.heading: Create a View
sections.body:
To create a view, you must use the [aggregation pipeline builder](atlas-ui-agg-builder). The output of your pipeline's final stage becomes the
content of the view.

To create a view from your pipeline results:

- Create an aggregation pipeline in the pipeline builder. For detailed
  instructions on using the pipeline builder, see
  [Create an Aggregation Pipeline](atlas-ui-create-agg-pipeline).

- Click the arrow next to the **Save** button at the top
  of the pipeline builder.

- Click **Create a View**.

- Enter a name for your view.

- Click **Create**.

Atlas creates a view from your pipeline results in the same
database where the pipeline was created.

sections.heading: Open a View
sections.body:
To open a view, either:

- Click the desired view from the
  [Collections screen](atlas-ui-collections-screen-views), or

- Click the desired view in the left-hand navigation.

After you open a view, Atlas shows you that view's [Document management](atlas-ui-documents). Atlas provides the following
information and functionality for the view:

- [Document management](atlas-ui-documents)

- [aggregation pipeline builder](atlas-ui-agg-builder)

- [Atlas UI](atlas-ui-indexes)

- [Data schema](atlas-ui-schema-tab)

- [Validation rules](atlas-ui-validation)

sections.heading: Duplicate a View
sections.body:
You can duplicate a view to modify an existing view while retaining the
original.

To duplicate a view:

- Hover over the desired view in the left navigation.

- Click the appearing **Ellipses (...)** button.

- In the drop-down menu, click **Duplicate View**.

- Enter a name for the new view.

- Click **Duplicate**.

sections.heading: Modify the Source of a View
sections.body:
> **Note:** 
>
> Considerations
>
> Views are read-only, and cannot inherently be modified. This
> procedure describes modifying the *underlying source* of a view.
>
> When you modify a view, Atlas cannot retain collation
> information associated with the view. Any collation information
> must be re-entered in the pipeline builder during modification.

To modify the source of a view:

- Hover over the desired view in the left navigation.

- Click the appearing **Ellipses (...)** button.

- In the dropdown, click **Modify view**. This
  button opens the [aggregation pipeline builder](atlas-ui-agg-builder)
  and populates the pipeline used to create the view.

- Modify the pipeline as desired. For detailed
  instructions on using the pipeline builder, see
  [Create an Aggregation Pipeline](atlas-ui-create-agg-pipeline).

- Click **Update View** at the top of the pipeline builder.

sections.heading: Drop a View
sections.body:
To drop a view from the database:

- Hover over the desired view in the left navigation.

- Click the appearing **Ellipses (...)** button.

- In the dropdown, click **Drop View**.

- In the modal, enter the name of the view.

- Click **Drop Collection**.
